chapter_title.ar: باب السَّهْوِ فِي الثَّلَاثِ وَ الْأَرْبَعِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 40 – The omission in the third and the fourth (Rak’at)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/193)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/193)
text: قَالَ إِنِ اسْتَوَى وَهْمُهُ فِي الثَّلَاثِ وَ الْأَرْبَعِ سَلَّمَ وَ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَ أَرْبَعَ سَجَدَاتٍ بِفَاتِحَةِ الْكِتَابِ وَ هُوَ جَالِسٌ يَقْصِدُ فِي التَّشَهُّدِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad, from Al Husayn, from Fazalat, from Al Husayn Bin Abu Al A’ala,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘If his imagination is established regarding the third and the fourth, he should greet (complete the Salāt), and he should pray two Rak’at of Salāt, and four Sajdah with the Opening of the Book while he is seated, intending in the Tashahhud’.
en.sarwar: 2. It is a narration from him (narrator of previous Hadith) by Ahmad ibn al-Husayn from Fadalah from al-Husayn ibn abu al-‘Ala’ who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one’s guess settles on his performing three or four Rak‘at, he says the phrase of offering greeting of peace, then performs two Rak‘at and four Sujud (prostrations) with al-Fatihah al-Kitab in a sitting position and says Tashahhud (testimonies of belief).”’
